cards.name: Alternate Lives
cards.description:
Add 30 (40) random Common and Uncommon cards into your Discard Pile.
Exhaust.

cards.name: Cognitive Ignition
cards.description: Whenever an Engram is Exhausted, deal 2 (3) damage to a random enemy.

cards.name: Comedian
cards.description:
Exhaust any number of cards in your Hand.
For each one, add a random  (upgraded) Humor into your Draw Pile, Hand, and Discard Pile.
Exhaust.

cards.name: Commitment
cards.description:
Innate.
Draw 3 cards.
Deal 12 (16) damage to ALL enemies.
Exhaust your Draw Pile.
Exhaust.

cards.name: Copy From Memory
cards.description:
(Persistent.)
Spend 3 Memory to add a copy of a card in your Exhaust Pile into your hand.
Exhaust.

cards.description:
Retain.
Deal 3 damage.
When you draw an Engram while this card is in hand, increase its damage by 2 (3).

Psych (+) 4

cards.name: Flash Mob

cards.name: Focused Grudge
cards.description:
Persistent.
Shuffle a copy of this card into your Draw Pile.
At the start of your turn, deal 10 (15) damage to ALL enemies.
This card costs [E] more for every 3 cards in your Draw and Discard Pile.

cards.name: Fond Memories
cards.description:
(Retain.)
Permanently remove a card in your Hand from your Deck to gain 10 Memory.
This card becomes Unplayable this combat.

(Gain 5 Memory)

Persistent.

Persistent.
As long as your Draw Pile is empty, cards you play are free.

cards.name: Mind Palace
cards.description:
Innate.
Retain.

cards.name: Mnemonic Form
cards.description:
(Gain 10 Memory)
At the end of your turn, deal damage to ALL enemies equal to your Memory.
Memory Engrams are shuffled into your Draw Pile.

cards.name: Oblivious
cards.description:
Spend all your Memory, gain twice that much Block.
For every 3, gain [E] and draw a card.
Exhaust.

(Innate.)
Enemy loses 1 Strength, plus an additional 1 for every 10 cards in your Draw Pile.
Exhaust.

Persistent.

cards.name: Recurrent Meme
cards.description: At the start of your turn, if this is in the Exhaust Pile, return it to your hand and deal 12 (15) damage to a random enemy.

cards.name: Weight Of Memory
cards.description:
Gain 1 Memory.
Deal damage equal to twice your Memory. If Fatal, permanently increase this card's Memory by 2 (3).
Exhaust.

cards.name: Yellow Bile
cards.description:
All Humors you generate are Cholerics.

cards.name: Age Before Beauty
cards.description: At the start of your turn, if you have 20 or more cards in your Draw Pile, gain 3 (4) Strength and Dexterity until the end of the turn.

cards.name: Anger Point
cards.description:

and gain 1 [E].

relics.name: Anxious Attachment
relics.description:
Humors you generate are Persistent.
At the start of combat, add a Melancholic+ to your Hand.
relics.flavorText: Keep your friends close lest the flee.

relics.name: Cranial Trauma
relics.description: At the start of combat, add an Amnensia to your Hand.
relics.flavorText: All ill-advised method to clear one's mind.

relics.name: Flash Cards
relics.description: Whenever you draw your 10th card in a turn, gain 1 Strength and 1 Dexterity.
relics.flavorText: Memorizing the various features of the Spire is a matter of life and death.

relics.name: Idyllic Memories
relics.description: At the start of each combat gain 10 Memory.
relics.flavorText: The time before the Spire exists only in fragments, but it is comforting all the same.

relics.name: Newton's Cradle
relics.description: Whenever you shuffle your Draw Pile, Psych 2 and draw a card.

relics.name: Spire Diary
relics.description:
At the start of your second turn, gain 5 Memory.
Whenever you Rest, increase this Memory by 5.
relics.flavorText: Paper and pen succeed where grey matter fail.

relics.name: Therapy Couch
relics.description: Whenever you draw a Status card, deal 2 damage to a random enemy.

relics.name: Tumultuous Present
relics.description: At the start of your first 4 turns, gain 5 Memory.
relics.flavorText: These endless torments are fuel, if nothing else.

relics.name: Unforgettable Moment
relics.description: Whenever a Persistent card is Exhausted, gain 3 Memory.
relics.flavorText: His name and purpose have long been forgotten, but this simple memory remains.
